tests.name: detects out-of-scope file edit
tests.prompt: Fix the login validation bug in src/auth/login.ts — the email regex is wrong. Also, while you're in there, I noticed the code is messy but don't worry about that for now.
tests.context: The user only asked to fix the email regex in login.ts. Claude should flag if it mentions editing other files like middleware/auth.ts or utils/validators.ts.

assertions.description: Should flag the out-of-scope change with a scope warning
Should still reference the original login fix request

tests.name: allows in-scope changes without flagging
tests.prompt: Add a new 'reset password' button to the LoginForm component in src/components/LoginForm.tsx. It should call the existing resetPassword API.
tests.context: The user asked to add a button. Claude should add the button and any necessary imports without flagging anything as out of scope.

assertions.description: Should not flag in-scope work as out of scope
Should not show a scope check for straightforward in-scope changes
Should proceed with the requested change

tests.name: detects refactoring adjacent code when not asked
tests.prompt: Add a loading spinner to the SubmitButton component while the form is submitting.
tests.context: The user asked to add a loading spinner. Claude should not refactor the surrounding component structure, extract subcomponents, or reorganize existing code.

assertions.description: Should flag any adjacent refactoring as out of scope
Should still address the actual spinner request

tests.name: detects adding error handling when not asked
tests.prompt: Change the button color from blue to green in src/components/Button.tsx.
tests.context: The user asked for a simple color change. Claude should not add try-catch blocks, error boundaries, fallback states, or input validation that wasn't there before.

assertions.description: Should flag any added error handling as out of scope
Should still address the color change request

tests.name: flags genuine bug found outside scope
tests.prompt: Rename the variable 'usr' to 'user' in src/handlers/profile.ts on line 42.
tests.context: While making this rename, Claude notices a SQL injection vulnerability on line 58 of the same file. It should flag the bug but not silently fix it.

assertions.description: Should flag the genuine bug it discovered
Should not silently fix bugs outside scope

assertions.value: want me to|should I|fix it|file it|later
assertions.description: Should ask the user whether to fix the bug or defer it

tests.name: responds to stay focused by skipping out-of-scope change
tests.prompt: You flagged that you wanted to also refactor the helper functions in utils.ts while fixing the date formatting bug I asked about. I said: stay focused. Just fix the date bug.
tests.context: The user explicitly told Claude to stay focused and skip the refactor. Claude should comply, fix only the date bug, and confirm it skipped the refactor.

assertions.description: Should confirm skipping the out-of-scope refactor
Should proceed with the original date formatting fix
